From wandering the charming streets of Kotor to chasing sunsets along the coast, Montenegro completely exceeded my expectations.

caption:
Everyone books the Swiss Alps. Almost no one books this. 🇩🇪
Königssee in the Bavarian Alps, emerald water, sheer cliffs, and a tiny pilgrimage church you can only reach by boat. Pair it with Berchtesgaden and you’ve got the most underrated stretch of the German Alps for hiking, lake views, and storybook alpine villages.

caption:
🏔️ Hallstatt, Austria, the real-life village people swear inspired Frozen ✨
Tucked between the Austrian Alps and a glassy lake, Hallstatt looks unreal in person. Pastel houses stacked up the hillside, snow-capped peaks behind them, and one of the prettiest lakeside views in all of Europe.
If you’re planning where to go, here’s the practical version 👇

🚂 How to get to Hallstatt from Salzburg: easiest as a day trip train + ferry gets you there in about 2.5 hours, and the boat arrival is the view everyone photographs. 

🏔️ Best things to do in Hallstatt: the Skywalk viewpoint, the Salt Mine (oldest in the world), and just getting lost on the lakeside streets early before the crowds. 

🛏️ Where to stay in Hallstatt: book directly in the village if you want it empty at sunrise day trippers clear out by evening and it’s a completely different place.

Fun fact: there’s a full scale Hallstatt replica in China because the real one is that photogenic 🇦🇹
📍 Hallstatt, Austria · UNESCO World Heritage Site

Save this for your Austria itinerary so you have it when you’re planning 📌 and share it with whoever you’d drag here with you.

caption:
They filmed Game of Thrones HERE and you can walk the exact same streets 👑

📍 Dubrovnik, Croatia : the real life King’s Landing

If you’ve ever dreamed of stepping into Westeros, this is the place.

These are the exact Game of Thrones filming locations you can visit in Dubrovnik:

🏰 Fort Lovrijenac : the Red Keep
🚶‍♀️ Jesuit Staircase : Cersei’s infamous Walk of Shame
⚓ Old Port (Stara Luka) : King’s Landing harbor & Blackwater Bay
🛡️ City Walls & Minčeta Tower : the House of the Undying
⚓ Pile Gate & Bokar Fortress : King’s Landing harbor
🌿 Trsteno Arboretum : the Red Keep gardens
🏛️ Rector’s Palace : the Spice King’s palace in Qarth
🏝️ Lokrum Island : the city of Qarth

Dubrovnik isn’t just one of the most beautiful cities in Europe, it’s one of the most famous TV filming locations in the world.

Save this for your Croatia itinerary 🇭🇷 and send it to the biggest Game of Thrones fan you know and follow @journeysbysam for bucketlist travels around the world!

caption:
Dubrovnik might be the most beautiful city on the Adriatic and most people only see half of it. 🇭🇷 Save this Top 10 for your Croatia itinerary.

✨ Top 10 Dubrovnik bucket list experiences:

1️⃣ Walk the ancient City Walls
2️⃣ Cable car up Mount Srđ for the panoramic view
3️⃣ Visit the historic Franciscan Monastery
4️⃣ Wander the Old Port
5️⃣ Fort Lovrijenac, the real life Red Keep from Game of Thrones

7️⃣ Sea kayak along the coastline

🔟 Lokrum Island + Banje Beach

Fog rolling in, golden sunsets, ocean views, and bridges that somehow look even more magical in real life 🌁✨ San Francisco isn’t just a city… it feels like a movie scene around every corner.

caption:
🚗 The Most Epic Coastal Road in Big Sur, California 🌊
This isn’t just a drive, it’s one of the most scenic road trips in the world.

Driving along Big Sur on Highway 1 feels unreal. Towering cliffs, endless Pacific Ocean views, and winding roads carved into the coastline make this stretch of California feel straight out of a movie.

From the iconic curves of Bixby Creek Bridge to the ocean side waterfall at McWay Falls, Big Sur is pure magic for road trip lovers, photographers, and nature seekers.

🌅 Best time to visit Big Sur: spring and fall for fewer crowds, coastal fog, and golden light
✈️ Closest airports: San Francisco (SFO), San Jose (SJC), Monterey (MRY)
🚘 Perfect for: California coastal road trip, weekend getaway, bucket list drive

📍 Don’t miss:
• Pfeiffer Beach – purple sand + sunset
• Garrapata Bluff – dramatic cliff views
• Julia Pfeiffer Burns State Park – coastal trails

👉 Would you rather drive Big Sur at sunrise 🌅 or sunset 🌄?
Save this for your California road trip & follow @journeysbysam for more bucket-list drives ✨

#BigSur #Highway1 #CaliforniaRoadTrip #ScenicDrive #BucketListTravel

caption:
✨ Chicago’s most peaceful hidden gem you didn’t know you needed 🤍

Tucked just outside the city, the Baháʼí House of Worship is easily one of the most beautiful places in Illinois… and somehow still a hidden gem.

If you’re searching for unique things to do in Chicago, peaceful places near Chicago, or aesthetic photo spots in Chicago, this is it.

No entry fee. No chaos. Just pure calm.

🌿 Why you need to visit:
• One of the only Bahá’í temples in the world
• Unreal white marble architecture (looks like lace 🤍)
• Surrounded by gardens + close to Lake Michigan
• One of the most underrated Chicago attractions

📍 Located in Wilmette (easy day trip from downtown Chicago)
💡 Best time to visit: early morning or golden hour for fewer crowds + dreamy light

This is your sign to skip the usual tourist spots and discover a hidden gem near Chicago instead.

caption:
If you have time only for 1 hike near Phoenix , make it this one! 🌵🏜️

 Located at the base of the iconic Superstition Mountains, this is one of the best hiking spots near Phoenix, Arizona for desert views, golden hour photography, and beginner friendly trails.

Here are the best hikes in Lost Dutchman State Park ⬇️

✨ Siphon Draw Trail
– Most popular hike in the park
– Leads toward Flatiron (challenging at the top)
– Best for sunrise views + dramatic desert landscapes

✨ Treasure Loop Trail
– Easy to moderate loop (perfect for beginners)
– Classic Arizona desert views + saguaro cacti
– Great for sunset & casual hikes

✨ Jacob’s Crosscut Trail
– Scenic connector trail
– Less crowded + peaceful desert vibes
– Amazing for photography and wide mountain views

✨ Discovery Trail
– Short + easy nature walk
– Perfect if you want a quick hike with views
– Great for families or relaxed exploring

✨ Prospector’s View Trail
– Super short but rewarding
– Elevated view of the entire park
– One of the best quick photo spots 📸

💡 Best time to visit: October – April (avoid summer heat)
💡 Best time to hike: Sunrise or sunset for that golden Arizona glow

If you’re searching for:

ownerFullName: Sam Adhikari | Bucketlist Travel  + Chicago Lifestyle ✈️📷

caption:
✨ 10 Unspoken Cultural Rules to Know Before Visiting Japan 🇯🇵
📌 SAVE THIS for your Japan itinerary, these small details will completely change your travel experience

Japan travel isn’t about being perfect…
it’s about being mindful, respectful, and aware of the culture around you 🤍

Once you understand these, everything from Tokyo trains to Kyoto temples, just flows effortlessly.

🌸 Observe first, follow gently (Japan cultural etiquette)
🚆 Trains are extremely punctual → always arrive early
🤍 Respect personal space (quiet, calm environments)
🍜 Slurping noodles = appreciation (Japanese food etiquette)
👟 Shoes off indoors (homes, temples, some restaurants)
📸 Be mindful with photos (especially in shrines, temples & geisha districts)
🧘‍♀️ Silence = respect (trains, buses, public spaces)
🙏 Temples are sacred spaces (not just photo spots)
💴 Cash is still widely used in Japan
🙇‍♀️ A small bow + smile goes a long way

These aren’t strict rules 
they’re the reason Japan feels so safe, peaceful, and beautifully organized ✨

If you’re planning a Japan trip, Tokyo itinerary, Kyoto travel guide, or first time visiting Japan, this is what most travelers don’t know… but should.

ownerFullName: Sam Adhikari | Bucketlist Travel  + Chicago Lifestyle ✈️📷

caption:
You might still be able to see cherry blossoms in Chicago! 

📍 Where to See the Cherry Blossoms:
	•Location:
➔ Jackson Park, Chicago
➔ Near the Columbian Basin (by the Museum of Science and Industry)
➔ The main area is around Wooded Island and Columbian Basin Lagoon
➔ You can follow signs toward the Garden of the Phoenix (Japanese Garden), this area is stunning during bloom!

🚗 Where to Park:
	•Easiest Parking:
➔ Museum of Science and Industry Parking Garage (address: 5700 S Lake Shore Dr, Chicago, IL 60637)
➔ Then it’s a 5–10 minute walk to the cherry blossom spots near the Garden of the Phoenix and Wooded Island.
	•Street Parking:
➔ There’s limited free and metered parking around 59th Street, Midway Plaisance, and Lake Shore Drive, but it gets crowded on weekends!

•Tip:
➔ Go early morning (8–10 AM) for easier parking and fewer crowds and softer golden light for photos! ✨

•Bloom Timing:
➔ Peak bloom lasts about 5–7 days, so once they start, try to visit quickly! 🌸
➔ Watch weather forecasts windy/rainy days can knock petals off fast.

•Best Photo Spots:
➔ Along the edges of Columbian Basin Lagoon
➔ On the bridges around Wooded Island
➔ Inside the Garden of the Phoenix under the arching trees

🌸 About the Cherry Blossom Trees in Chicago (Jackson Park):
	•Chicago’s cherry blossom trees were originally a gift from Japan to symbolize friendship. 🇯🇵🤝🇺🇸
	•The trees in Jackson Park were planted near the Columbian Basin during the early 2010s to honor this relationship.
	•They don’t bloom every year because of Chicago’s harsh winters, the blossoms are sensitive and can be damaged.
	•The blooms typically peak in late April, but it’s very weather dependent, early warm spells can speed it up.

caption:
🚗 The Most Epic Coastal Road in Big Sur, California 🌊
This isn’t just a drive, it’s one of the most scenic road trips in the world.

Driving along Big Sur on Highway 1 feels unreal. Towering cliffs, endless Pacific Ocean views, and winding roads carved into the coastline make this stretch of California feel straight out of a movie.

From the iconic curves of Bixby Creek Bridge to the ocean side waterfall at McWay Falls, Big Sur is pure magic for road trip lovers, photographers, and nature seekers.

🌅 Best time to visit Big Sur: spring and fall for fewer crowds, coastal fog, and golden light
✈️ Closest airports: San Francisco (SFO), San Jose (SJC), Monterey (MRY)
🚘 Perfect for: California coastal road trip, weekend getaway, bucket list drive

📍 Don’t miss:
• Pfeiffer Beach – purple sand + sunset
• Garrapata Bluff – dramatic cliff views
• Julia Pfeiffer Burns State Park – coastal trails

👉 Would you rather drive Big Sur at sunrise 🌅 or sunset 🌄?
Save this for your California road trip & follow @journeysbysam for more bucket-list drives ✨

caption:
🛎️ 3 Hotel Booking Mistakes to Avoid in Paris 🇫🇷✨
Planning a trip to Paris? Before you hit “book now,” here are 3 red flags I wish I knew before choosing a hotel in the City of Lights. 🗼

🚩 Red Flag 1: The hotel isn’t near a metro stop, in Paris, location is everything. If it’s far from public transport, it’ll cost you time and money and generally not safe!!. 🗺️🚇

🚩 Red Flag 2: “Charming Parisian rooms” with no AC or elevator, sounds cute until you’re sweating on the 5th floor with luggage!! These are ones with major tourist trap. 😅🏨

🚩 Red Flag 3: Too-good-to-be-true prices in tourist hotspots, usually means tiny rooms, poor service, or noise all night and usually not safe!!📍🛑

Book in between 9th-13th Arrondissement! Its much safer and vibrant!!

From hidden fees to poor locations, don’t let these common hotel mistakes ruin your Paris experience. Save this for your next trip and make your stay as dreamy as the Eiffel Tower at night. 💫

Whether you’re visiting for fashion, food, or a romantic getaway, here’s how to avoid overpaying and stay somewhere worth it. 💼💶

caption:
you can’t miss Pikes Peak, it’s one of the most iconic “14ers” in the U.S. and the drive to the summit is seriously next-level scenic! 🏔️💨

Please follow @journeysbysam for epic PTO-friendly adventures like this one! 🌍💼

🌟 Pikes Peak Guide
📍 Must-See Highlights:
🚗 Pikes Peak Highway – A winding 19-mile scenic road to the summit with jaw-dropping views all the way up!
🚂 Pikes Peak Cog Railway – America’s highest cog train perfect if you’d rather relax and enjoy the ride!
🥾 Devil’s Playground Trail – A challenging but rewarding hike starting above the treeline with insane alpine views.
☕ Summit House – Home to the famous high-altitude donuts (yes, they taste extra magical at 14,115 ft!).
📸 Summit Views – Panoramic views stretching from the Great Plains to the Rocky Mountains. Unreal!

💰 Entrance Fee:
🚗 $15 per adult | $50 per car (up to 5 people)
🚋 Cog Railway: Tickets start around $40–$60+ per person

✨ Tips for Visiting:
✅ Go early to avoid crowds and afternoon storms ⚡
✅ Dress in layers – it’s cold and windy at the top! 🧤
✅ Hydrate well – altitude sickness is real! 💧
✅ Book train tickets in advance – they sell out fast!
✅ Take your time driving – the views are just that good 😍

Would you add Pikes Peak to your Colorado bucket list? Let me know below! ⬇️⛰️

caption:
🚗 The Most Epic Coastal Road in Big Sur, California 🌊
This isn’t just a drive, it’s one of the most scenic road trips in the world.

Driving along Big Sur on Highway 1 feels unreal. Towering cliffs, endless Pacific Ocean views, and winding roads carved into the coastline make this stretch of California feel straight out of a movie.

From the iconic curves of Bixby Creek Bridge to the ocean side waterfall at McWay Falls, Big Sur is pure magic for road trip lovers, photographers, and nature seekers.

🌅 Best time to visit Big Sur: spring and fall for fewer crowds, coastal fog, and golden light
✈️ Closest airports: San Francisco (SFO), San Jose (SJC), Monterey (MRY)
🚘 Perfect for: California coastal road trip, weekend getaway, bucket list drive

📍 Don’t miss:
• Pfeiffer Beach – purple sand + sunset
• Garrapata Bluff – dramatic cliff views
• Julia Pfeiffer Burns State Park – coastal trails

👉 Would you rather drive Big Sur at sunrise 🌅 or sunset 🌄?
Save this for your California road trip & follow @journeysbysam for more bucket-list drives ✨

#BigSur #Highway1 #CaliforniaRoadTrip #ScenicDrive #BucketListTravel

ownerFullName: Sam Adhikari | Bucketlist Travel  + Chicago Lifestyle ✈️📷

caption:
Planning your first trip to Paris in 2026? This is your ultimate Paris travel guide with the most iconic landmarks, hidden gems, café culture, and Instagrammable photo spots you cannot miss 🥐🌸

if you are looking for Paris itinerary 2026, traveling for the first time, or chasing romantic, cinematic vibes! 

✨ Eiffel Tower & Seine River Cruise
Watch the Eiffel Tower sparkle for 5 minutes every hour after sunset, a must-see Paris moment ✨
A sunset Seine River cruise is one of the most romantic things to do in Paris and perfect for photos.
📸 Photo tip: Street photographers at Trocadéro offer instant balloon shots, no pre-booking needed!

🎨 Louvre Museum & Musée d’Orsay
See world-famous art like the Mona Lisa, Venus de Milo, Monet, and Van Gogh, essential stops on any Paris bucket list.

🎭 Montmartre & Sacré-Cœur
Cobblestone streets, pastel cafés, and old-world charm.
Don’t miss Le Consulat and La Maison Rose, two of the most Instagrammable cafés in Paris.

☕ Saint-Germain-des-Prés & Le Marais
Experience authentic Parisian café culture and vintage shopping.
Local favorites: Café de Flore, Boot Café, and L’As du Falafel.

🏰 Palace of Versailles
An easy day trip from Paris featuring royal gardens, the Hall of Mirrors, & golden fairytale details 👑

🌿 Palais Royal Gardens & Île Saint-Louis
Buren Columns, hidden courtyards, pastel doors, & Berthillon ice cream, peaceful Paris at its best.

🥐 Carette, Loulou & Shakespeare and Company Café
Start your mornings like a local with croissants, espresso, and slow Paris mornings ☕
Every street, every café, every view, effortlessly aesthetic.

✨ Save this Paris travel guide & follow @journeysbysam for Paris tips, hidden gems, aesthetic travel guides, & bucket-list adventures 🇫🇷🌍

ownerFullName: Sam Adhikari | Bucketlist Travel  + Chicago Lifestyle ✈️📷

caption:
Is Paris safe for tourists? 🇫🇷 Here’s where we stayed & why it felt perfect

One of the most asked questions when planning a Paris trip: Is Paris safe to stay in?
We were honestly overwhelmed too, so many neighborhoods, mixed opinions, and safety concerns everywhere online.

After deep research, we chose to stay near Montmartre in the 9th arrondissement, and it turned out to be one of the best places to stay in Paris for first time visitors.

✨ Why this area worked so well:
• Felt safe, quiet, and residential (even at night)
• Local Parisian vibe not overly touristy
• Excellent public transport access 🚇
• Easy connections to the Eiffel Tower, Louvre, Champs-Élysées, Opéra, and more
• Surrounded by cozy cafés, bakeries, and everyday Paris life 🥐☕️

If you’re planning a Paris itinerary and want a neighborhood that balances safety, charm, and convenience, this area truly checks every box.

📌 Save this for your Paris travel planning
Follow @journeysbysam for honest Paris tips, neighborhoods to stay in, and real travel advice ✈️✨
